metaBuilds.title: On-Hit DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 398 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +26% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Storm Breaker (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Murdock deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Eden ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 37% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Drongo deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Revenant ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Wraith ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. TwinBlast ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Inner Fury” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field carry 52.9% over 447,813 games, magnitude not simulated.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 30% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 41% of your damage.
